snapshot-style assertions with pretty_assertions in some scanner tests","linting":"rustfmt with repo rustfmt.toml, clippy run with -D warnings in CI, cargo deny for license/dependency auditing","error_handling":"thiserror for library-level typed errors (ForgeError, ScannerError enums with #[error] variants), anyhow::Result at CLI boundary, no panics/unwraps in library crates outside tests, ? propagation with From impls between error types"},"patterns":["Forge trait with async_trait methods (list_repos, get_file_contents, post_comment, list_webhooks) implemented per-provider, injected via Box<dyn Forge> or generic bound","Rule-based SecurityScanner that iterates a Vec<Box<dyn Rule>> or enum-dispatched rules over a normalized Finding/Match struct decoupled from raw forge payloads","Config uses a tagged enum (forge_type: github|gitlab|...) deserialized with serde, feeding a factory function that returns the right Forge impl","Builder pattern for constructing scanner instances with optional rule sets","Feature flags in Cargo.toml gating optional forge backends to keep default build lean"],"related_issues":[{"number":735,"title":"feat(core): add GitLab forge backend (initial multi-forge groundwork)","relevance":"introduced the first non-GitHub Forge trait implementation and exposed gaps in the trait's assumptions about PR vs MR terminology and pagination"},{"number":736,"title":"PR: implement GitLab Forge trait impl + config plumbing","relevance":"merged PR showing the concrete pattern (module layout, test harness, config enum) to replicate for Gitea/Forgejo and Azure DevOps"},{"number":1050,"title":"refactor(core): normalize Forge trait around generic PullRequest/MergeRequest struct","relevance":"prior refactor abstracting terminology differences between forges; any forge-specific normalization has to happen at the client/adapter layer, not inside scanner rules","Backward compatibility: existing GitHub-only configs must continue to work without requiring a forge_type field (default to github)","Azure DevOps API differs structurally from REST-based GitHub/GitLab (uses OData-style querying and PAT auth with different header scheme), requiring a distinct client rather than reusing the generic reqwest wrapper as-is","Gitea and Forgejo share near-identical APIs (Forgejo is a Gitea fork) so a single backend with capability detection is preferable to duplicating code","CI matrix and feature flags must be updated to build/test each forge backend independently to avoid bloating default compile time"],"test_coverage":"GitHub forge has solid integration coverage (github_forge_test.rs) using wiremock to stub REST responses for repo listing, file fetch, and comment posting; extend config enum and factory function; reuse existing client.rs with an added AuthScheme enum for PAT/basic auth support needed by Azure DevOps","pros":["Follows proven pattern from #735/#736, minimal risk of architectural drift","Smaller PRs possible, one forge backend at a time","Reuses existing test harness (wiremock) with minimal changes"],"cons":["Trait may accumulate provider-specific optional methods over time (e.g., MR vs PR terminology) leading to leaky abstraction","Azure DevOps auth differences force changes to shared client.rs, coupling unrelated backends"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":9},{"name":"Introduce a capability-based Forge abstraction with adapter layer","description":"Refactor Forge trait into a smaller core trait plus optional capability traits (e.g., WebhookCapable, CommentCapable), and add a normalization adapter layer that maps each provider's PR/MR/webhook payload into a shared domain model before it reaches SecurityScanner; implement Gitea/Forgejo as a single backend with a forge_flavor field, and Azure DevOps as a separate client using its own auth/request module","pros":["Cleanly resolves SecurityScanner's implicit GitHub coupling (addresses issue #980-style bugs) as part of this feature","Scales better for future forges since capabilities are opt-in","Gitea/Forgejo share one implementation, reducing duplication"],"cons":["Larger refactor touching scanner, config, and CLI wiring simultaneously","Higher review overhead and risk of regressions in the well-tested GitHub path","Requires updating all existing tests to the new trait shape"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":18}
